AMD.reasoning: MACRO REGIME: Mixed-to-cautious. The prior risk-on baseline (Iran peace talks, S&P 500 ATH 7,230 on May 1) has reversed as of May 4: reports of missile strikes on a US warship near Jask island have reignited Strait of Hormuz closure risk, pushed oil higher, and pulled Dow futures lower. Fed is on hold at 3.5-3.75% with a leadership transition (Powell out May 15, Warsh as likely successor) introducing additional policy uncertainty. This is a transitional-to-risk-off regime, not the clean risk-on backdrop that justifies chasing a +71% 21-day move. RELATIVE STRENGTH: AMD has massively outperformed — ROC 21d=+71.51%, ROC 10d=+29.51%. RSI-14=83.49, RSI-21=86.42, ADX=76.53. Price is 53% above the 50-SMA (z-score 2.5) and at Bollinger pct_b=0.91 — extreme stretch by any measure. While outperformance signals sector inflows, this is LATE in a sector rotation cycle, not early, which is the trigger condition for my macro veto. The wiki thesis explicitly flags: 'Four consecutive runs produced the highest momentum readings in the watchlist. The stock has now run +74% in 21 trading days to $354 ATH.' Entering at all-time highs on day 22 of a 74% run is chasing, not leading. Hourly momentum is already decelerating: ROC 5d=-2.56%, ROC 10d=-1.3%. Hourly OBV is diverging from price (bearish divergence). Hourly MACD histogram at -0.02 (flat/turning). ASYMMETRY MATH: Upside to Fib ext 127.2% = $409 (~13.7% from $360). Nearest hourly support = $328.81 (~8.8% downside). Conservative stop near $328 = ~8.8% risk. 13.7% upside vs 8.8% downside = 1.55:1 asymmetry — below the 2:1 minimum bar. If price flushes to $310 on an earnings miss (the fat-tail risk with AMD reporting May 5 AMC), downside expands to ~14%, flipping asymmetry negative. BINARY EVENT OVERRIDE: AMD reports Q1 earnings tomorrow May 5 after market close. This is a regime-dominating binary event that makes the asymmetry calculation unreliable — the stock can gap 15-20% in either direction overnight, instantly invalidating any stop. The wiki flags this explicitly: 'Earnings on May 5 create a binary event that dominates near-term risk.' Entering a swing position the day before earnings at all-time highs with RSI 83 violates capital preservation discipline. LESSONS FLAG: The most recent lessons entry (NVDA, 2026-04-30) is a direct analogue: RSI 81-87, bullish AI capex thesis, macro stress — result was a stop-out. I am not repeating that setup without adjustment. PASS CRITERIA: The setup fails on (1) asymmetry below 2:1 threshold, (2) binary earnings event tomorrow making stops unreliable, (3) late-cycle relative-strength entry (day 22 of +74% move), (4) macro regime is cautious/transitional not clean risk-on. POST-EARNINGS OPPORTUNITY: If AMD beats and dips to $316-$328 (wiki-flagged entry zone: 10-EMA / Fib 23.6% confluence), the asymmetry and timing improve materially — that would be the correct entry window with ~20% upside to $409 vs ~5% downside to $310.
AMD.timeframe: 5-10 trading days (post-earnings, contingent on May 5 AMC reaction)
